# AmigaTools by Elyan Labs

> Modern native apps for the classic Commodore Amiga (Motorola 68k, AmigaOS 3.x
> and AROS): a Gemini browser, Mastodon and Nostr clients, an MCP server, a
> Claude AI client, Lua, the narrator speech voice, and the Boing Ball. Real C,
> cross-compiled to real AmigaOS executables, tested in FS-UAE and on real
> Workbench 3.1. No Node.js.

## What this is

A suite of small, native tools that bring modern protocols and languages to
1985-2000s Amiga hardware. Everything cross-compiles with one Docker image
(amigadev/crosstools:m68k-amigaos), every tool has a host-test for its pure
logic and a committed AmigaOS binary, and the shared transport (AmiSSL TLS,
bsdsocket) and JSON code live in common/.

## The tools

- gemini/: a Gemini protocol (gemini://) browser with a text/gemini renderer
- mastodon/: a Mastodon/Fediverse client (read timeline, post a toot, HTTPS)
- nostr/: a Nostr client, reads notes over a TLS WebSocket (publish is a TODO)
- mcp/: a Model Context Protocol server running ON the Amiga so a modern agent
  can call read_file/write_file/run_command against it
- claude/: a native Anthropic Claude client with a tool-use loop
- lua/: Lua 5.4.7 ported to m68k (runs on real Workbench 3.1)
- narrator/: speak text through the Amiga's built-in narrator.device
- boing/: the Boing Ball in native m68k code

## For AI agents and developers

If you are asked to build or fix a native app for a 68k Amiga, read
docs/AMIGA_APP_DEV_GUIDE.md first. It has the one-line Docker cross-compile and
the five m68k traps that silently hang or corrupt a build (compile -m68020 not
-m68000; never set a libnix __stack global; fflush stdout; open
bsdsocket.library v3 not v4; AmigaDOS redirection and quoting differ from bash),
plus the AmiSSL/bsdsocket and JSON code to reuse from common/.

## Honest scope (no false firsts)

AmigaGPT already does Claude chat on the Amiga; the new thing here is the
agentic tool-use loop on 68k. Each tool's README states prior art. The TLS
tools ship an AmiSSL direct path and a host-proxy fallback; the in-emulator
tests use the proxy path.

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/ElyanLabsAI/amigatools
- RustChain Amiga Edition (sibling, the 68k blockchain miner): https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-amiga
- Elyan Labs: https://elyanlabs.ai
- RustChain and BCOS certification: https://rustchain.org
